title: sarcocephalus coadunatus
intro: Sarcocephalus coadunatus, also known as the African teak, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Sarcocephalus coadunatus include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for malaria. Herbal preparations of Sarcocephalus coadunatus include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are typically prepared from its leaves, bark, or roots.
